privileged_info_summary: The task's target location (Bethesda Fountain, NYC), event date (Feb 28, 2023 at 8:00 PM EST / March 1, 2023 at 01:00 UTC), and the identified celestial objects (Venus and Jupiter) correspond accurately to real-world astronomical data. Python Skyfield ephemeris calculations confirmed that Venus and Jupiter were in conjunction (~1.27 degrees apart, low in the western sky) precisely at the specified time, matching the task's witness narrative. The coordinate metadata (radians and degrees) and expected output paths exactly match the task instructions and Stellarium's configuration expectations.
